trip.name: Italy + Switzerland Mar 2026
trip.budget: Confirmed: ~RM 6,670 + miles flights | Outstanding: Rome hotel + Italy trains + attraction tickets

days.title: Arrive Riomaggiore
days.subtitle: Nice → Cinque Terre · Check in Le Giare

timeline.title: Train from Nice → Riomaggiore
timeline.detail: Scenic coastal route. VALIDATE ticket before boarding — €50 fine otherwise. 2 large bags: take the main road up from station, NOT the stairs.

timeline.title: Check in: Affittacamere Le Giare
timeline.detail: Via Colombo 71. ✅ PAID RM819. Key from host. Leave heavier bag — you won't need it until Mar 20 checkout.

timeline.title: Walk Via dell'Amore
timeline.detail: 15min coastal path to Manarola. Best photos at golden hour 17:30–18:30. Walk back before dark — no lighting.

timeline.title: ☕ Arrival drink: Bar & Vini A Pié de Ma'
timeline.detail: Right at the bottom of the train stairs. Espresso €1.20, local white wine €3. Best spot to shake off the journey. Has focaccia + local anchovies.
timeline.mapQuery: Bar A Pié de Ma Riomaggiore

timeline.title: 🐟 Dinner: Il Pescato Cucinato
timeline.detail: Best value in Cinque Terre. Fried anchovies, calamari, mixed fish plate €10–14pp. Queue outside, eat standing at the counter or nearby steps. Cash only.

highlights: 🌅 Golden hour at Via dell'Amore 17:30–18:30
🐟 Fried anchovies standing at Il Pescato Cucinato
🍷 Sciacchetrà wine with sea view at Cantina
localTips: Crowds thin massively after 17:00 — arrive afternoon, not morning
Bags too heavy for trails — leave main luggage at Le Giare (host allows)
ATM at the train station. No big ATMs in village.
Cell reception spotty on Via dell'Amore — download offline maps
Riomaggiore is the quietest village at night — best base for sleep

days.title: Cinque Terre Full Day
days.subtitle: Monterosso · Blue Trail hike · Vernazza · Manarola

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Bar & Vini A Pié de Ma'
timeline.detail: Cornetto (croissant) €1.30 + cappuccino €1.40. Pack a focaccia slice for the hike (€2). Eat fast — first train to Monterosso at 08:30.
timeline.mapQuery: Bar A Pie de Ma Riomaggiore

timeline.title: Buy Cinque Terre Card at station
timeline.detail: €18.50/person — trails + unlimited train between villages. Start north (Monterosso) and work south.

timeline.title: Train to Monterosso al Mare
timeline.detail: 10min, every ~20min. The only village with a real sandy beach.

timeline.title: Monterosso Old Town + Limoncino
timeline.detail: Focaccia at Forno Pasticceria Flavia €2. Free limoncino tasting at Il Carugio. Buy a pesto jar €4.

timeline.title: 🥾 Hike Monterosso → Vernazza
timeline.detail: 90min moderate. Most scenic stretch of Blue Trail. Bring water. Views of the entire coastline arc.

timeline.title: 🦐 Lunch: Vernazza harbour steps
timeline.detail: Buy focaccia at Focacceria Vernazza (€2.50) + sit on the harbour steps. OR Gambero Rosso restaurant: spaghetti vongole €16, grilled fish €18. Book Gambero Rosso if you want proper sit-down.

timeline.title: Train to Manarola
timeline.detail: Skip Corniglia (382-step climb). Manarola = most photogenic village. 5min train.

timeline.title: 📸 Manarola panoramic viewpoint
timeline.detail: Follow signs to 'Punto Panoramico' (10min walk above village). Best light 16:00–18:00. Stay until dark — the village lights are extraordinary.

timeline.title: 🍝 Dinner: Trattoria La Grotta
timeline.detail: Riomaggiore. Inside a cave — genuinely unique setting. Pesto trofie €13, grilled sea bass €18, house Vermentino €14/bottle. Reserve ahead or arrive before 19:00. Best dinner in Cinque Terre.

highlights: 🥾 Monterosso → Vernazza hike (90min, most scenic stretch)
📸 Manarola Punto Panoramico at 16:00 for best light
🍝 Dinner in a cave at Trattoria La Grotta
localTips: Buy Cinque Terre Card first (€18.50) — cheaper than individual train tickets
Skip Corniglia unless you're fit — 382 steps from the train
Vernazza harbour lunch beats all restaurants on price + vibe
Trails can close after rain — check parconazionale5terre.it morning of
Last train back to Riomaggiore runs around 23:00 — don't stress

days.title: Cinque Terre → Varenna
days.subtitle: Morning swim · IC 666 LGLXHN · Lake Como arrival

timeline.title: Last swim, Riomaggiore beach
timeline.detail: Rocky beach below the harbour. No crowds at 07:00. Last sea swim before Alpine Switzerland.

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Riomaggiore station bar
timeline.detail: Quick espresso + cornetto before REG 22910 at 09:55. €2.50 total. Nothing fancy — fuel stop.

timeline.title: ⚠️ Pack + check out Le Giare
timeline.detail: Pack EVERYTHING the night before. Walk 5min to station on FLAT road — do NOT take stairs with large bags.

timeline.title: 🚂 REG 22910: Riomaggiore → Levanto
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Departs Platform 1. Arrive Levanto 10:20. Follow signs to IC 666 platform.

timeline.title: 🚂 IC 666 (PNR: LGLXHN): Levanto → Milan Centrale
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED €76.80. Coach 7: Alex Seat 10A, Khai Yee Seat 10B. Show PNR LGLXHN to conductor + valid ID. Air-conditioned. Arrive Milan 13:59.

timeline.title: 🍕 Lunch: Milano Centrale food hall
timeline.detail: You have 20min in Milan (14:20 train). Head to the lower level food hall — Spontini pizza al taglio (€4/slice, legendary in Milan) or grab a tramezzino for the train. Fast, excellent.

timeline.title: 🚂 REG 2828 (Trenord): Milan → Varenna-Esino
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Ticket code 2778108890. No reserved seats. Check big departure board for platform at Centrale. Arrive Varenna 15:24.

timeline.title: Check in: Hotel Olivedo Varenna
timeline.detail: ✅ PAID RM671. Lakefront. 2min from ferry pier. Drop bags, walk straight to the terrace. Como in evening light is extraordinary.

timeline.title: 🌿 Villa Cipressi — lakeside gardens
timeline.detail: Right next to Hotel Olivedo — 2 min walk. Historic 1400s villa, terraced gardens to the water. Cypress, magnolia, stone balustrades. €4pp walk-in. 30–40min. Romantic and quiet — almost no tourists.

timeline.title: 🚣 Private boat rental — just the two of you
timeline.detail: Barche a Noleggio at the Varenna pier — small motorboat €25–35/hr, no licence needed. Cut the engine mid-lake and drift. Mirror-flat water in late afternoon, Alps reflected. Most romantic hour of the trip. Return by 18:00.
timeline.mapQuery: Varenna boat rental Lake Como pier

timeline.title: 🚶 Passeggiata degli Innamorati
timeline.detail: The 'Walk of Lovers' — narrow lit path right on the waterfront, hugging the lake edge. Bellagio's lights starting to reflect on the Como water across from you. Alpine mountains silhouetting against the dusk sky. 15–20 min slow walk. Completely free. Most romantic spot in Varenna.

timeline.title: 🌿 Villa Cipressi gardens (evening stroll)
timeline.detail: If still open — check the gate on your way past. Even if closed, the terrace view from outside is lovely at dusk. Worth the 2 min detour from the Passeggiata.

timeline.title: 🏘️ Varenna old town evening wander
timeline.detail: Medieval lanes light up warmly at night. Walk Via Venini and the old centre — stone archways, hanging lanterns, locals out for their passeggiata. No agenda — just soak it in. This is real Italian lake life.

timeline.title: 🥂 Aperitivo: Nilus Bar
timeline.detail: Lakeside terrace, order a Negroni or Aperol Spritz €5–6. Sit facing the water — Bellagio's lights now fully reflected on the lake. Como mountains as a dark silhouette behind. Perfect 30 min wind-down before dinner.

timeline.title: 🍝 Dinner: Il Cavatappi
timeline.detail: Via XX Settembre 10. Risotto al lago (perch fillet) €15, tagliatelle with wild mushrooms €13, lake fish €18. Cosy stone interior, dim lighting — perfect romantic end to the day. If you haven't called ahead: +39 0341 815349. Ask for a window table.

highlights: 🚶 Passeggiata degli Innamorati at dusk — Bellagio lights on the water
🥂 Nilus Bar Negroni — mountains silhouetting behind the lake at night
🍝 Il Cavatappi dinner — stone interior, lake fish, full romantic evening
localTips: Villa Monastero is closed (under renovation) — confirmed. Villa Cipressi is the alternative right next door.
Boat rental: 'Barche a Noleggio' signs at the pier. Go after Cipressi, late afternoon water is calmest.
Hotel Olivedo terrace: ask to sit outside for pre-dinner drinks if Nilus is crowded.
Il Cavatappi: book ahead tonight — +39 0341 815349. Only ~8 tables.
Varenna closes early — most shops shut by 20:00. Buy prosecco before the boat if you want drinks on the water.

days.title: Lake Como → Lucerne
days.subtitle: Actual late arrival · 22:41 check-in night · sleep only

timeline.title: Late rail journey to Luzern
timeline.detail: Actual arrival is late tonight after the missed earlier connection. Priority is simple: get to ibis, check in, hot shower, sleep. No sightseeing tonight.

timeline.title: Arrive Luzern
timeline.detail: Get off at Luzern, walk/taxi to ibis, and do the minimum only. No pressure to explore tonight — tomorrow is now a recovery day.

timeline.title: Check in: ibis Styles Luzern City
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED RM1,117 (3 nights). Drop bags, charge devices, sleep immediately.

highlights: 🚂 Safely reach Luzern tonight
🛏️ Proper rest after long travel day
✅ ibis Luzern already booked
localTips: Tonight is not a sightseeing night — optimize for sleep.
Set out only what you need for tomorrow morning.
Keep passports and tickets together before bed.

days.title: Lucerne Historic Day
days.subtitle: Sleep in · Musegg Wall → Old Town loop · Lion Monument · Swiss dinner

timeline.title: Breakfast: Heini Luzern
timeline.detail: Start easy with pastries and coffee before the old-town loop. Order a butter gipfeli or nut pastry + coffee and keep it light after yesterday’s late arrival.

timeline.title: Musegg Wall + Zytturm
timeline.detail: Lucerne’s preserved medieval rampart. Built as part of the city fortifications after Lucerne joined the Swiss Confederation in 1332; the Zytturm clock is famous for being allowed to chime one minute before all other city clocks.

timeline.title: Walk down into Altstadt via Weinmarkt
timeline.detail: Weinmarkt was one of medieval Lucerne’s civic and trading squares. This old town core grew when Lucerne became an important route between northern Europe and the St Gotthard pass into Italy.

timeline.title: Lunch: Wirtshaus Galliker
timeline.detail: Best lunch anchor for today’s Swiss-city plan. Order one classic Swiss plate: veal sausage, schnitzel or rösti. This is the right stop for substance, not just scenery.

timeline.title: Chapel Bridge → Jesuit Church → Reuss river walk
timeline.detail: Kapellbrücke dates to the 14th century and is one of Europe’s oldest covered wooden bridges; the Jesuit Church nearby is considered Switzerland’s first large Baroque church. This section shows Lucerne’s shift from medieval trading town to Catholic Baroque city.

timeline.title: Lion Monument + coffee break
timeline.detail: Carved in 1820–21 after a design by Bertel Thorvaldsen, the dying lion commemorates the Swiss Guards killed defending Louis XVI during the 1792 Tuileries attack in Paris. It is one of Lucerne’s most important memorials, not just a photo stop.

timeline.title: Optional Spreuer Bridge / Old Town final loop
timeline.detail: Spreuerbrücke is another historic wooden bridge, rebuilt in the 16th century after flood damage. It is known for its painted Dance of Death panels — a very different mood from the more famous Chapel Bridge.

timeline.title: Dinner: Restaurant Pfistern
timeline.detail: Finish with a proper Swiss old-town dinner in a historic setting. Order rösti or Zürcher Geschnetzeltes and keep it an early, easy finish.

highlights: 🗺️ Route order: Heini → Musegg → Weinmarkt → Galliker → Chapel Bridge → Lion Monument → Pfistern
🏰 Musegg Wall first = strongest non-lake Lucerne experience
🥘 Order Swiss classics at Galliker: veal sausage / schnitzel / rösti
🍽️ Pfistern dinner: rösti or Zürcher Geschnetzeltes in the old town
localTips: Start at Musegg so the day feels urban-medieval, not lakeside-romantic again.
Keep the river/bridge section compact — do not let the day drift into another waterfront repeat of Como.
Galliker is the best lunch anchor if you want a classic Lucerne meal, not a generic tourist stop.
If energy drops after Lion Monument, cut Spreuer Bridge and go straight to dinner at Pfistern.

days.title: Lucerne · Grindelwald by train
days.subtitle: Swiss alpine village day · green mountain views · village first, First if weather good

timeline.title: Breakfast + head to Luzern station
timeline.detail: Keep breakfast easy and be at Luzern station before 09:00. Train is the right call — no mountain-road stress, no parking issues, better use of the day.

timeline.title: 🚆 PE LIX: Luzern → Interlaken Ost
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Point-to-point ticket via Brünig – Interlaken Ost. Luzern 09:06 → Interlaken Ost 10:54 on PE LIX. Covered by SBB order 151420339818. Return is also covered on the same date ticket.

timeline.title: 🚆 R 61: Interlaken Ost → Grindelwald
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Interlaken Ost 11:04 → Grindelwald Terminal 11:40 on R 61. Ticket valid Luzern ↔ Grindelwald Terminal until 24 Mar 05:00.

timeline.title: Grindelwald village first
timeline.detail: Do not rush straight into cable-car mode. Walk the village first — this is where you get the chalet atmosphere, green valley feel and classic Swiss alpine setting you wanted.

timeline.title: Lunch in Grindelwald
timeline.detail: Have a proper village lunch before deciding on the uplift. Pick whatever is open and highly rated on the day — check same-day status before committing.

timeline.title: Optional: Grindelwald First if weather is good
timeline.detail: If visibility is good, go up for the stronger alpine payoff. If weather is weak, stay in the village and keep the day focused on atmosphere instead of forcing a cable-car add-on.

timeline.title: Start return toward Luzern
timeline.detail: Aim for a late-afternoon return so the day feels full but not exhausting before the Venice transfer day.

highlights: 🚆 Real timetable checked: 09:06 Luzern → 10:54 Interlaken Ost → 11:40 Grindelwald
🏔️ Best fit for alpine village atmosphere + green mountain scenery
🏡 Village first, First only if weather is worth it
localTips: Train is better than driving for tomorrow — less stress, more scenery, no parking hassle.
Treat Grindelwald as the whole day, not a base to cram multiple destinations.
If cloud is poor, enjoy the village and skip forcing First.
Check same-day opening status before choosing lunch.

days.title: Lucerne → Venice
days.subtitle: Train through Gotthard · Grand Canal arrival · First night

timeline.title: Checkout ibis Luzern
timeline.detail: Pack everything. Last Swiss breakfast. Station luggage lockers available if needed.

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Migros bakery, Lucerne Hbf
timeline.detail: Grab a Gipfeli + coffee at the station Migros (opens 06:30). CHF 5 total. Stock up on Swiss chocolate for the train — last chance for Swiss prices.

timeline.title: 🚂 Luzern → Arth-Goldau → Venezia S. Lucia
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED CHF242.20 for 2. IR 2017: Luzern 08:39 Platform 4 → Arth-Goldau 09:11 Platform 6. Then EC 31: Arth-Goldau 09:18 Platform 3 → Venezia S. Lucia 14:42. Coach 3, seats 35 + 36. SBB order 151419996814. Trenitalia PNR RWXTWN. Keep PDF + ID ready.

timeline.title: 🥪 Lunch: On the train (packed) or Lugano
timeline.detail: Best option: pack lunch from Migros. Alternative: 20min stop in Lugano — Bottegone del Vino near station for quick wine + antipasti CHF 15. Train has bistro car — sandwiches CHF 9.

timeline.title: Arrive Venice Santa Lucia
timeline.detail: Station opens directly onto Grand Canal — the arrival is spectacular. DO NOT take a taxi boat. Walk to your hotel first, then explore light.

timeline.title: Check in: Palazzo Cendon - Il Piano Nobile
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED RM917.10 total (2 nights, ~RM458.55/night). Cannaregio. Better character than the budget chain options, still within target budget.
timeline.mapQuery: Palazzo Cendon Il Piano Nobile Venice

timeline.title: 🌉 Walk to Rialto Bridge
timeline.detail: Free. 30min walk east. Best photo from Fondamenta del Vin. Rialto was Venice’s commercial heart for centuries — the bridge marks the historic crossing of the Grand Canal where merchants, banks and market life concentrated.

timeline.title: 📸 San Marco at sunset
timeline.detail: Piazza free. Basilica entry €3. Campanile tower €10. This was the ceremonial and political center of the Venetian Republic — the place where Venice staged its power to the world.

timeline.title: 🦞 Dinner: All'Arco, Venice (cicchetti)
timeline.detail: Best cicchetti bar in Venice — Calle dell'Arco near Rialto Market. Bite-size snacks €1.50–3 each — baccalà mantecato, sarde in saor, meatballs. Order 6–8 pieces + ombra wine (€1.50/glass). Real Venice experience.

highlights: 🚂 SBB to Venice Santa Lucia via Milan — scenic Alpine entry into Italy
🚤 First vaporetto ride: Line 2 from Ferrovia → Rialto
🌃 Rialto Bridge at night — lit up, almost no tourists after 22:00
localTips: Palazzo Cendon is your booked Venice stay — check the flattest luggage route from Santa Lucia on Maps on arrival.
Buy Vaporetto pass only if you actually plan multiple boat rides — Venice is very walkable.
Don’t take a water taxi from the station — too expensive for the value.
Cannaregio gives you a more local Venice feel than the San Marco zone.
Refill bottles at public fountains — Venice has drinkable water.

days.title: Venice Full Day
days.subtitle: Murano · Burano · Doge's Palace · Gondola at sunset

timeline.title: 🚢 Vaporetto to Murano + Burano
timeline.detail: Line 12 from Fondamente Nove. Murano became the center of Venetian glassmaking after furnaces were moved off the main islands in 1291 due to fire risk; Burano developed its own fishing village identity and lace tradition.

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Pasticceria Tonolo
timeline.detail: Best pastry shop in Venice. Dorsoduro 3764. Croissant + cappuccino €3.50. Sfogliatelle, tiramisu, bomboloni — all exceptional. Tiny, no seats — stand at the bar like Venetians. Opens 07:45.

timeline.title: 🦐 Lunch: All'Arco (or Rialto Market)
timeline.detail: Return to All'Arco near Rialto for cicchetti lunch OR buy from the Rialto fish market stalls — cured meats, cheese, artichokes, €8–12 for a full spread. Eat on the market steps.

timeline.title: 🏛️ Doge's Palace
timeline.detail: €25pp — book online. This was the political heart of the Venetian Republic: the Doge’s residence, senate chambers, courts and state bureaucracy all in one complex. Bridge of Sighs is inside.

timeline.title: 🎭 Handmade masks — Laboratorio Artigiano
timeline.detail: Only buy true papier-mâché craft masks. Venice’s mask tradition comes from the republic’s carnival culture, when masks blurred class boundaries and allowed anonymity in public life.

timeline.title: 🚤 Gondola at sunset
timeline.detail: €80/30min daytime, €100 evening. Gondolas were once everyday transport for Venetian elites; today they survive as one of the last visible symbols of Venice’s canal-based urban life.

timeline.title: 🎭 Dinner: Osteria Alle Testiere
timeline.detail: Venice's best small restaurant — only 24 seats. Book 2 weeks ahead (testiere.it). Tagliolini with shrimp €18, grilled turbot €32, wine pairing €20/person. Worth every euro — you'll remember this meal.

highlights: 🏛️ St Mark's Basilica free entry (skip the paid 'no-queue' — arrive 09:00)
🎭 Rialto Market 08:00–12:00 — best food photography in Italy
🌅 Punta della Dogana sunset — Giudecca Canal, free, extraordinary
localTips: Avoid St Mark's Square restaurants — €18 for a cappuccino with cover charge
Acqua alta (flooding): Nov–Mar risk, check comune.venezia.it for warnings
Best cicchetti (tapas) street: Calle dei Boteri near Rialto — €1–2 per bite
Murano glass factories: free tours at Venini or Davide Penso (no purchase required)
San Polo and Cannaregio sestieri = real Venice locals, 80% fewer tourists than San Marco

days.title: Venice → Florence
days.subtitle: Frecciarossa 10:26 · Duomo quarter · Piazzale Michelangelo sunset

timeline.title: San Polo sestiere morning walk
timeline.detail: Away from tourists. Scuola Grande di San Rocco (Tintoretto, €10). Campo Santa Margherita market: local coffee, focaccia €1.50.

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Venice Santa Lucia station bar
timeline.detail: Quick espresso + tramezzino (crustless sandwich, €2.50) at the station before Frecciarossa. Bar right inside departures. Nothing special — fuel stop only.

timeline.title: Walk to Santa Lucia station
timeline.detail: Allow 20–30min with bags depending on bridge route from Palazzo Cendon. Leave buffer and use Google Maps walking mode.

timeline.title: 🚄 Frecciarossa: Venice → Florence SMN
timeline.detail: ⏳ BOOK TRENITALIA. 2hr. €40–55pp. 1st class only ~€10 more — worth it. Arrive Florence Santa Maria Novella 12:28.

timeline.title: Check in: La Grancontessa
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED RM965.80 total for 2 nights (~RM482.90/night). Good-value Florence stay under your RM500/night target.

timeline.title: 🏛️ Piazza della Signoria
timeline.detail: Free. Neptune Fountain, Perseus by Cellini, replica David. This square was the civic stage of republican Florence and later Medici rule — effectively the political heart of the city.

timeline.title: 🥩 Lunch: Mercato Centrale, Florence
timeline.detail: Arrive Florence ~12:30. Head straight to Mercato Centrale upstairs food hall (Via dell'Ariento). Best lunch in Florence — lampredotto (tripe) sandwich €5, pasta al ragù €8, fresh pizza €6. Local crowd, no tourists upstairs.

timeline.title: 🌉 Ponte Vecchio
timeline.detail: Free. Florence’s oldest bridge, famous for the shops built along it. The current structure largely dates to the 14th century and survived World War II unlike many other European historic bridges.

timeline.title: 🌅 Piazzale Michelangelo at sunset
timeline.detail: Walk 20min uphill or Bus 13. Built in the 19th century as part of Florence’s urban redesign, it is not ancient — but it gives the best overview of the Renaissance city and the Arno curve.

timeline.title: 🍽️ Dinner: Trattoria Mario, Florence
timeline.detail: Via Rosina 2, since 1953. Cash only, communal tables, no menu — waiter tells you what's available. Ribollita €7, bistecca Fiorentina (600g) €35, house red €8/litre. Arrive at 19:45 for first seating — queue otherwise.

highlights: 🚄 Frecciarossa 10:26 → Florence SMN, 2h10min
🌸 Piazzale Michelangelo: first Florence panorama, walk up from station (~30min)
🥩 Bistecca Fiorentina tonight — minimum 600g, wood-fire, non-negotiable
localTips: Florence Santa Maria Novella station: luggage storage €6/bag per day (lower level)
Piazzale Michelangelo: sunset 18:00–19:00 in March — packed but worth it
La Grancontessa: Via Porta Rossa, 3min walk to Uffizi and Duomo
First night: eat at Trattoria Mario for the Florentine experience (cash only)
Validate train ticket or use QR — Frecciarossa crew checks every seat

days.title: Florence Full Day
days.subtitle: Uffizi · Duomo Cupola · Accademia · David

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Caffè Gilli
timeline.detail: Historic café since 1733, Piazza della Repubblica. Cornetto di crema €2.20, cappuccino €2. Stand at the bar (not the tables — table service doubles the price). Art Nouveau interior. Instagram moment.

timeline.title: 🖼️ Uffizi Gallery
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED for 27 Mar at 09:15. Total €58. The Uffizi began as Medici administrative offices (uffizi = offices) in the 16th century before becoming one of the world’s great Renaissance art collections.

timeline.title: 🥗 Lunch: Buca Mario
timeline.detail: Oldest restaurant in Florence (1886). Via Porta Rossa. Pappardelle al cinghiale €14, ribollita €9, grilled Chianina beef €22. More formal than Mario — but the history and vaulted ceilings are worth it.

timeline.title: ⛪ Duomo complex
timeline.detail: Cupola climb €18. Brunelleschi’s dome solved one of the great engineering problems of the Renaissance and became the architectural symbol of Florence’s ambition and wealth.

timeline.title: 👨‍🎨 Accademia — Michelangelo's David
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED for 27 Mar at 16:00. Total €48 for 2 adults. David was carved by Michelangelo from a difficult block of marble and became a symbol of Florentine republican pride, not just a masterpiece of anatomy.

timeline.title: 🛍️ Leather + souvenirs
timeline.detail: Scuola del Cuoio (leather school, quality). Procacci on Via de' Tornabuoni: truffle paste €8, best Florence food gift.

timeline.title: 🥩 Dinner: Trattoria Sostanza
timeline.detail: Famous for butter rigatoni (rigatini al burro) and T-bone steak. Via della Porcellana 25. The butter pasta is life-changing — €12. Bistecca Fiorentina €55 for 2 (shared, minimum 1kg). Book ahead.

highlights: 🎨 Uffizi Gallery: Botticelli's Birth of Venus + Primavera (Room 10–14)
🗿 Accademia: Michelangelo's David — book slot in advance or queue 2h
🌉 Ponte Vecchio at 07:00 — only time you get it to yourself
localTips: Uffizi: book online at uffizi.it (€3 booking fee) — worth it, saves 1–2h queue
David at Accademia: €12 + booking fee — book same day on accademia.org
Mercato Centrale upstairs: best lunch in Florence, €8–12 all local
Gelato truth: avoid waffle cones with piled-up colours — look for covered metal trays (artisan)
Duomo climb is free with Florence Card — top view rivals Rome from above

days.title: Florence → Rome
days.subtitle: Frecciarossa south · Trastevere · Trevi · Spanish Steps · Night 1 of 3

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Florence hotel or SMN station
timeline.detail: Quick breakfast at hotel or grab a cornetto at the Santa Maria Novella station bar before the Frecciarossa. €2.50. Keep it light — Rome has a big lunch waiting.

timeline.title: 🚄 Frecciarossa: Florence → Roma Termini
timeline.detail: ⏳ BOOK TRENITALIA. 1hr 33min. €30–45pp. Arrive 12:03.
timeline.mapQuery: Florence Santa Maria Novella Train Station

timeline.title: Check in: Hotel Artemide
timeline.detail: ⏳ BOOK NOW. Via Nazionale. Good base for a 3-night Rome stay, near Termini and Repubblica.

timeline.title: 🍕 Lunch: Pizzarium Bonci, Rome
timeline.detail: Near Vatican (Via della Meloria). Gabriele Bonci's legendary pizza al taglio — sold by weight, €5–8 for a satisfying portion. Best pizza bianca in Rome. Eat standing outside.

timeline.title: 🏘️ Trastevere walk
timeline.detail: Cross Tiber via Ponte Sisto. Trastevere was historically a working-class district outside Rome’s ancient core and still retains a more lived-in, neighborhood feel than the monumental center.

timeline.title: ⛲ Trevi Fountain
timeline.detail: Go at quieter hours for fewer crowds. The current fountain is 18th century, built where an ancient Roman aqueduct — the Aqua Virgo — terminated, linking Baroque Rome to imperial infrastructure.

timeline.title: 🏛️ Spanish Steps at sunset
timeline.detail: 135 steps, completed in the 1720s to connect the Bourbon Spanish embassy below with the French church above — a very Roman mix of diplomacy, urban theater and prestige.

timeline.title: 🍝 Dinner: Ristorante Roscioli
timeline.detail: Via dei Giubbonari 21. Rome's best pasta — tonnarelli cacio e pepe €16, bucatini all'amatriciana €14. Enormous wine cellar. Book at least 1 week ahead (roscioli.com). If full: Felice a Testaccio (same quality, easier booking).

timeline.title: 🌙 Piazza Navona evening
timeline.detail: Free. The long oval shape comes from the Stadium of Domitian beneath it, so you are effectively standing on the footprint of an ancient Roman arena reworked into Baroque Rome.

highlights: 🚄 Frecciarossa Florence SMN → Roma Termini ~1h30min
🏛️ First view: walk from Termini to Piazza della Repubblica (15min)
🍜 First Roman meal: Tonnarelli cacio e pepe at Roscioli or Felice a Testaccio
localTips: Roma Termini area sketchy — hotel Artemide is 400m away, safe neighbourhood
Buy metro ticket before exiting Termini: €1.50/ride or €7 day pass
Evening walk: Via del Corso → Trevi Fountain → Pantheon in sequence, ~2km total
Trevi Fountain at 23:00 is magical and half the daytime crowd
Hotel Artemide tip: ask for a higher floor room for city rooftop views

days.title: Rome Full Day
days.subtitle: Colosseum · Roman Forum · Vatican · Pantheon

timeline.title: ☕ Breakfast: Caffè Sant'Eustachio
timeline.detail: Piazza di Sant'Eustachio 82 — best espresso in Rome, full stop. Gran caffè speciale €2.20 (pre-sweetened — ask 'senza zucchero' if you want it plain). No seats — stand at bar. 5min walk from Pantheon.

timeline.title: 🏟️ Colosseum — 8am first slot
timeline.detail: ⚠️ BOOK official site. Opened in AD 80 under Titus, the Colosseum was the Flavian amphitheater — Rome’s great arena for spectacles, imperial propaganda and mass entertainment.

timeline.title: 🏛️ Roman Forum + Palatine Hill
timeline.detail: Included in combo. The Forum was the political, religious and legal center of ancient Rome, while the Palatine became the hill of imperial palaces and elite power.

timeline.title: 🫙 Lunch: Terre e Domus, Roman Forum view
timeline.detail: Via Sacra (near Forum entrance). Rooftop terrace with direct views of the Colosseum and Forum. Cacio e pepe €14, supplì (fried rice balls) €4 each, local Frascati white €6/glass. Worth the slight premium for the view.

timeline.title: ⛪ Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel
timeline.detail: ⚠️ BOOK official site. The museums are the accumulated papal collections; the Sistine Chapel matters not just for Michelangelo’s ceiling, but because it remains the site of the papal conclave.

timeline.title: ⛪ St Peter's Basilica (free)
timeline.detail: No booking needed. Built over the traditional tomb of St Peter, this is one of the central churches of Catholicism and a statement of papal power in architecture.

timeline.title: 🌉 Castel Sant'Angelo at dusk
timeline.detail: €15. Originally Emperor Hadrian’s mausoleum, later converted into a papal fortress and refuge — one building that compresses imperial Rome, medieval defense and papal politics together.

timeline.title: 🍽️ Dinner: Da Enzo al 29, Trastevere
timeline.detail: Via dei Vascellari 29. Tiny, 10 tables, always full — book on their site. Cacio e pepe €13, coda alla vaccinara (oxtail stew) €14, carciofi alla romana €7. Authentic Romans eat here. One of Rome's best.

highlights: 🏟️ Colosseum + Roman Forum + Palatine Hill: single ticket, full morning
🕍 Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel: book early morning slot (opens 09:00)
🌙 Trastevere dinner: authentic Roman neighbourhood, no tourist menu boards
localTips: Colosseum: book at coopculture.it — ticket includes Forum + Palatine (€22)
Vatican Museums: 3.5h minimum including Sistine Chapel — book online in advance
Skip the 'skip the line' touts outside Colosseum — they sell the same ticket at markup
Trastevere: Da Enzo al 29 (cacio e pepe €13), Da Augusto for outdoor tables
If only one gelato in Rome: Il Gelato di San Crispino near Trevi — no cones, bowls only

days.title: Rome → Fly Home
days.subtitle: Mar 31 · FCO departure day · SQ365 + SQ132 · booked return to Penang

timeline.title: Final Rome breakfast + check out
timeline.detail: Keep the morning simple and efficient. Pack the night before and stay focused on the airport transfer.

timeline.title: Leonardo Express: Roma Termini → FCO
timeline.detail: Use Leonardo Express to avoid stress. Allow generous buffer for long-haul departure and terminal formalities.

timeline.title: ✈️ SQ365: Rome FCO → Singapore SIN
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Depart Fiumicino Terminal 3 at 11:40 Tue 31 Mar. Arrive Singapore 06:00 Wed 1 Apr. Economy Advantage on Airbus A350-900. 30kg checked baggage each.

timeline.title: ✈️ SQ132: Singapore SIN → Penang PEN
timeline.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Depart Changi Terminal 2 at 08:00 Wed 1 Apr. Arrive Penang 09:25. Economy Advantage on Boeing 737-8 MAX. 2h connection in Singapore.

highlights: ✈️ Return flight home now booked
🎫 Singapore Airlines via Singapore, 30kg baggage each
🧳 Departure day should stay low-stress and simple
localTips: Do not schedule sightseeing on departure day.
Keep passport, chargers, and liquids packed the night before.
Aim to arrive FCO with generous buffer.

hotels.note: Via Colombo 71 · Sea views · 5min walk from station

hotels.name: Hotel Olivedo
hotels.note: Lakefront · 2min to ferry pier · Historic building

hotels.name: Rome hotel near Termini / Repubblica
hotels.note: Agoda search · Mar 28–31 · 2 adults · under RM500/night target

items: VALIDATE regional train tickets BEFORE boarding at yellow/green machines on the platform — €50 fine if not validated. Frecce (Frecciarossa) tickets: no validation needed.
IC/Frecce trains: always reserve a seat. Regional trains (REG/RE): no reservation required.
Trenitalia app: buy Frecce tickets up to 4 months ahead — 'Super Economy' fares are 60% cheaper than walk-up.
Milan Centrale platforms: check the departure board only at T-30min. Boards update late — do not assume your platform.
Trenord (Milan commuter trains to Varenna): separate ticket from Trenitalia. Buy at the Trenord counter at Centrale.
IC 666 PNR LGLXHN: show this code to the on-board conductor when they come around. Have your passport ready.
Swiss trains leave on the exact minute — 14:53 means 14:53, not 14:54. Be on the platform 3min before.
SBB app: best rail app in the world. Real-time platform info, seat reservations, e-tickets.
CHF = Swiss Franc. ≈ same value as EUR. Prices are higher but quality matches everywhere.
Supermarkets (Migros, Coop): buy lunch here. CHF 8 vs CHF 30+ restaurant. Locals do it. No shame.
Tap water in Switzerland is among the world's best. Refill from any public stone fountain in towns — free.
Passports needed at Italian–Swiss border (Chiasso). Have them accessible on the train.
Blue Trail (Sentiero Azzurro): check which sections are open at the station — some close seasonally.
Cinque Terre Card (€18.50): covers all trails AND unlimited trains between villages for the day.
Vernazza and Corniglia are less touristy than Riomaggiore and Monterosso — prefer them for lunch.
Anchovies (alici): the local CT speciality. Fresh from the sea. Order in every form.
Sciacchetrà: sweet passito wine (sun-dried grapes). Very special, very local, €8/glass. Worth it.
Pesto alla Genovese: the real version uses a marble mortar, not a blender. Only buy from producers.
Via dell'Amore: the coastal love path between Riomaggiore and Manarola. Partially open seasonally — check locally.
Varenna is the least-touristy major Como village — preferred over Bellagio and Menaggio for staying.
Lake perch (pesce persico): the regional freshwater fish unique to Como. Order it wherever you see it.
Ferries vs cars: trains + ferries dominate Como. Cars = terrible traffic. Never rent a car on Lake Como.
Villa del Balbianello: arrive at 09:00 on the dot for the best light before tour groups arrive.
Villa Melzi in Bellagio: better garden than Balbianello in some ways, less famous, rarely crowded.
FAI membership at Balbianello: if you have one, book online. Otherwise walk in and pay contribution at gate.
Vaporetto 24hr pass €25 — buy at machines outside Santa Lucia station before going anywhere.
Water taxi: €80+ minimum. Gondola: exactly €80 (30min, daytime) — official rate, non-negotiable.
NEVER eat anywhere with photos on the menu or a man standing outside beckoning. Tourist tax.
The real Venice is in the sestieri away from San Marco: Cannaregio, Dorsoduro, Castello. Get lost.
Acqua alta (high water) in March: check Comune di Venezia forecast. Keep waterproof shoes/sandals accessible.
Cicchetti (bacari): Venice's tapas bars. Standing, €1–2 per bite. This is how locals eat. Al Marca near Rialto.
Gondola: best launch from small back canals near Rialto, NOT from Grand Canal (too trafficky).
Do Cliff Walk FIRST before altitude tiredness sets in. Goes fast with groups — go early.
Glacier Cave temperature: ~0°C inside even in summer. Bring a layer in your bag.
Ice Flyer chairlift (CHF 20 extra): completely open, flies over glacier crevasses. Only in clear weather — worth it.
Trübsee lake: stop on descent at intermediate station. In March it's frozen — extraordinary photo.
Engelberger Klosterkäse from the monastery shop: made by Benedictine monks. Best Swiss souvenir.
Book Titlis gondola online for 10% discount. Bring the booking QR code — saves the ticket desk queue.
Uffizi: spend 75% of time in Rooms 10–14 (Botticelli) and Room 15 (Leonardo). Everything else is bonus.
The real David is dramatically different from photos — the scale hits you entering the room. Don't rush it.
Bistecca Fiorentina rule: minimum 800g, served rare (al sangue). Restaurants may refuse if asked well-done.
Real gelato test: stored in metal containers with lids, never piled high in coloured mounds.
Mercato Centrale: ground floor = legendary food stalls. Top floor restaurants = tourist trap. Ground only.
Best Florence sunset: Ponte Santa Trinita (not Piazzale). Ponte Vecchio in frame, far fewer people.
Colosseum 8am slot is significantly less crowded than 10am. Hypogeum add-on: worth every cent.
Vatican entrance: Viale Vaticano (museum entrance) — NOT St Peter's Square. Separate queues.
Dress code enforced at Vatican and all major churches: covered shoulders AND knees both.
Roman espresso at the bar: €1. At a table: €3–5. Always stand at the bar.

The Three Roman Pasta Laws: Cacio e Pepe (no cream), Carbonara (no cream — egg + guanciale only), Amatriciana (no onion — guanciale + tomato only). Any restaurant breaking these laws is tourist-grade.
Carciofi alla Romana (braised artichoke): only available Feb–April. It's spring — order this.
Supplì: Roman fried rice balls €1.50. Order as antipasto everywhere. Always.
Italy: always carry €30 cash — small markets, trattorias, cicchetti bars are cash only.
Best exchange: Wise card loaded with EUR + CHF before departure. Zero foreign transaction fees.
Tipping Italy: not mandatory. Round up or leave €2–3 for good service. Never 15–20% like US.
Water: say 'acqua del rubinetto' (tap water, free) or 'acqua naturale' (still bottled) in restaurants.
Espresso ordering: 'un caffè' = espresso. 'cappuccino' only before 11am (cultural rule). 'Caffè macchiato' = espresso + tiny foam.
Swiss tipping: round up to nearest CHF. Service charge (servizio incluso) usually included in bill.
Emergency numbers: Italy 112 (police), 118 (ambulance). Switzerland 117 (police), 144 (ambulance).
Italian phrases: 'Buongiorno' (good morning/formal), 'grazie' (thank you), 'il conto per favore' (the bill please), 'dov'è la stazione?' (where is the station?).

stays.note: Lakefront · 2min to ferry pier · Historic building

stays.name: Palazzo Cendon - Il Piano Nobile
stays.note: Cannaregio · Character stay · Under RM500/night target

stays.name: Rome hotel near Termini / Repubblica

segments.title: Train from Nice → Riomaggiore
segments.detail: Scenic coastal route. VALIDATE ticket before boarding — €50 fine otherwise. 2 large bags: take the main road up from station, NOT the stairs.

segments.title: Buy Cinque Terre Card at station
segments.detail: €18.50/person — trails + unlimited train between villages. Start north (Monterosso) and work south.

segments.title: Train to Monterosso al Mare
segments.detail: 10min, every ~20min. The only village with a real sandy beach.

segments.title: Train to Manarola
segments.detail: Skip Corniglia (382-step climb). Manarola = most photogenic village. 5min train.

segments.title: ⚠️ Pack + check out Le Giare
segments.detail: Pack EVERYTHING the night before. Walk 5min to station on FLAT road — do NOT take stairs with large bags.

segments.title: 🚂 REG 22910: Riomaggiore → Levanto
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Departs Platform 1. Arrive Levanto 10:20. Follow signs to IC 666 platform.

segments.title: 🚂 IC 666 (PNR: LGLXHN): Levanto → Milan Centrale
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED €76.80. Coach 7: Alex Seat 10A, Khai Yee Seat 10B. Show PNR LGLXHN to conductor + valid ID. Air-conditioned. Arrive Milan 13:59.

segments.title: 🚂 REG 2828 (Trenord): Milan → Varenna-Esino
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Ticket code 2778108890. No reserved seats. Check big departure board for platform at Centrale. Arrive Varenna 15:24.

segments.title: Late rail journey to Luzern
segments.detail: Actual arrival is late tonight after the missed earlier connection. Priority is simple: get to ibis, check in, hot shower, sleep. No sightseeing tonight.

segments.title: Arrive Luzern
segments.detail: Get off at Luzern, walk/taxi to ibis, and do the minimum only. No pressure to explore tonight — tomorrow is now a recovery day.

segments.title: 🚆 PE LIX: Luzern → Interlaken Ost
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Point-to-point ticket via Brünig – Interlaken Ost. Luzern 09:06 → Interlaken Ost 10:54 on PE LIX. Covered by SBB order 151420339818. Return is also covered on the same date ticket.

segments.title: 🚆 R 61: Interlaken Ost → Grindelwald
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Interlaken Ost 11:04 → Grindelwald Terminal 11:40 on R 61. Ticket valid Luzern ↔ Grindelwald Terminal until 24 Mar 05:00.

segments.title: Start return toward Luzern
segments.detail: Aim for a late-afternoon return so the day feels full but not exhausting before the Venice transfer day.

segments.title: 🚂 Luzern → Arth-Goldau → Venezia S. Lucia
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED CHF242.20 for 2. IR 2017: Luzern 08:39 Platform 4 → Arth-Goldau 09:11 Platform 6. Then EC 31: Arth-Goldau 09:18 Platform 3 → Venezia S. Lucia 14:42. Coach 3, seats 35 + 36. SBB order 151419996814. Trenitalia PNR RWXTWN. Keep PDF + ID ready.

segments.title: Arrive Venice Santa Lucia
segments.detail: Station opens directly onto Grand Canal — the arrival is spectacular. DO NOT take a taxi boat. Walk to your hotel first, then explore light.

segments.title: Walk to Santa Lucia station
segments.detail: Allow 20–30min with bags depending on bridge route from Palazzo Cendon. Leave buffer and use Google Maps walking mode.

segments.title: 🚄 Frecciarossa: Venice → Florence SMN
segments.detail: ⏳ BOOK TRENITALIA. 2hr. €40–55pp. 1st class only ~€10 more — worth it. Arrive Florence Santa Maria Novella 12:28.

segments.title: 🚄 Frecciarossa: Florence → Roma Termini
segments.detail: ⏳ BOOK TRENITALIA. 1hr 33min. €30–45pp. Arrive 12:03.
segments.mapQuery: Florence Santa Maria Novella Train Station

segments.title: Leonardo Express: Roma Termini → FCO
segments.detail: Use Leonardo Express to avoid stress. Allow generous buffer for long-haul departure and terminal formalities.

segments.title: ✈️ SQ365: Rome FCO → Singapore SIN
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Depart Fiumicino Terminal 3 at 11:40 Tue 31 Mar. Arrive Singapore 06:00 Wed 1 Apr. Economy Advantage on Airbus A350-900. 30kg checked baggage each.

segments.title: ✈️ SQ132: Singapore SIN → Penang PEN
segments.detail: ✅ BOOKED. Depart Changi Terminal 2 at 08:00 Wed 1 Apr. Arrive Penang 09:25. Economy Advantage on Boeing 737-8 MAX. 2h connection in Singapore.

activities.title: Check in: Affittacamere Le Giare
activities.detail: Via Colombo 71. ✅ PAID RM819. Key from host. Leave heavier bag — you won't need it until Mar 20 checkout.

activities.title: Walk Via dell'Amore
activities.detail: 15min coastal path to Manarola. Best photos at golden hour 17:30–18:30. Walk back before dark — no lighting.

activities.title: Monterosso Old Town + Limoncino
activities.detail: Focaccia at Forno Pasticceria Flavia €2. Free limoncino tasting at Il Carugio. Buy a pesto jar €4.

activities.title: 🥾 Hike Monterosso → Vernazza
activities.detail: 90min moderate. Most scenic stretch of Blue Trail. Bring water. Views of the entire coastline arc.

activities.title: 📸 Manarola panoramic viewpoint
activities.detail: Follow signs to 'Punto Panoramico' (10min walk above village). Best light 16:00–18:00. Stay until dark — the village lights are extraordinary.

activities.title: Last swim, Riomaggiore beach
activities.detail: Rocky beach below the harbour. No crowds at 07:00. Last sea swim before Alpine Switzerland.

activities.title: Check in: Hotel Olivedo Varenna
activities.detail: ✅ PAID RM671. Lakefront. 2min from ferry pier. Drop bags, walk straight to the terrace. Como in evening light is extraordinary.

activities.title: 🌿 Villa Cipressi — lakeside gardens
activities.detail: Right next to Hotel Olivedo — 2 min walk. Historic 1400s villa, terraced gardens to the water. Cypress, magnolia, stone balustrades. €4pp walk-in. 30–40min. Romantic and quiet — almost no tourists.

activities.title: 🚣 Private boat rental — just the two of you
activities.detail: Barche a Noleggio at the Varenna pier — small motorboat €25–35/hr, no licence needed. Cut the engine mid-lake and drift. Mirror-flat water in late afternoon, Alps reflected. Most romantic hour of the trip. Return by 18:00.
activities.mapQuery: Varenna boat rental Lake Como pier

activities.title: 🚶 Passeggiata degli Innamorati
activities.detail: The 'Walk of Lovers' — narrow lit path right on the waterfront, hugging the lake edge. Bellagio's lights starting to reflect on the Como water across from you. Alpine mountains silhouetting against the dusk sky. 15–20 min slow walk. Completely free. Most romantic spot in Varenna.

activities.title: 🌿 Villa Cipressi gardens (evening stroll)
activities.detail: If still open — check the gate on your way past. Even if closed, the terrace view from outside is lovely at dusk. Worth the 2 min detour from the Passeggiata.

activities.title: 🏘️ Varenna old town evening wander
activities.detail: Medieval lanes light up warmly at night. Walk Via Venini and the old centre — stone archways, hanging lanterns, locals out for their passeggiata. No agenda — just soak it in. This is real Italian lake life.

activities.title: Check in: ibis Styles Luzern City
activities.detail: ✅ BOOKED RM1,117 (3 nights). Drop bags, charge devices, sleep immediately.

activities.title: Musegg Wall + Zytturm
activities.detail: Lucerne’s preserved medieval rampart. Built as part of the city fortifications after Lucerne joined the Swiss Confederation in 1332; the Zytturm clock is famous for being allowed to chime one minute before all other city clocks.

activities.title: Walk down into Altstadt via Weinmarkt
activities.detail: Weinmarkt was one of medieval Lucerne’s civic and trading squares. This old town core grew when Lucerne became an important route between northern Europe and the St Gotthard pass into Italy.

activities.title: Chapel Bridge → Jesuit Church → Reuss river walk
activities.detail: Kapellbrücke dates to the 14th century and is one of Europe’s oldest covered wooden bridges; the Jesuit Church nearby is considered Switzerland’s first large Baroque church. This section shows Lucerne’s shift from medieval trading town to Catholic Baroque city.

activities.title: Lion Monument + coffee break
activities.detail: Carved in 1820–21 after a design by Bertel Thorvaldsen, the dying lion commemorates the Swiss Guards killed defending Louis XVI during the 1792 Tuileries attack in Paris. It is one of Lucerne’s most important memorials, not just a photo stop.

activities.title: Optional Spreuer Bridge / Old Town final loop
activities.detail: Spreuerbrücke is another historic wooden bridge, rebuilt in the 16th century after flood damage. It is known for its painted Dance of Death panels — a very different mood from the more famous Chapel Bridge.

activities.title: Grindelwald village first
activities.detail: Do not rush straight into cable-car mode. Walk the village first — this is where you get the chalet atmosphere, green valley feel and classic Swiss alpine setting you wanted.

activities.title: Optional: Grindelwald First if weather is good
activities.detail: If visibility is good, go up for the stronger alpine payoff. If weather is weak, stay in the village and keep the day focused on atmosphere instead of forcing a cable-car add-on.

activities.title: Checkout ibis Luzern
activities.detail: Pack everything. Last Swiss breakfast. Station luggage lockers available if needed.

activities.title: Check in: Palazzo Cendon - Il Piano Nobile
activities.detail: ✅ BOOKED RM917.10 total (2 nights, ~RM458.55/night). Cannaregio. Better character than the budget chain options, still within target budget.
activities.mapQuery: Palazzo Cendon Il Piano Nobile Venice

activities.title: 🌉 Walk to Rialto Bridge
activities.detail: Free. 30min walk east. Best photo from Fondamenta del Vin. Rialto was Venice’s commercial heart for centuries — the bridge marks the historic crossing of the Grand Canal where merchants, banks and market life concentrated.

activities.title: 📸 San Marco at sunset
activities.detail: Piazza free. Basilica entry €3. Campanile tower €10. This was the ceremonial and political center of the Venetian Republic — the place where Venice staged its power to the world.

activities.title: 🚢 Vaporetto to Murano + Burano
activities.detail: Line 12 from Fondamente Nove. Murano became the center of Venetian glassmaking after furnaces were moved off the main islands in 1291 due to fire risk; Burano developed its own fishing village identity and lace tradition.

activities.title: 🏛️ Doge's Palace
activities.detail: €25pp — book online. This was the political heart of the Venetian Republic: the Doge’s residence, senate chambers, courts and state bureaucracy all in one complex. Bridge of Sighs is inside.

activities.title: 🎭 Handmade masks — Laboratorio Artigiano
activities.detail: Only buy true papier-mâché craft masks. Venice’s mask tradition comes from the republic’s carnival culture, when masks blurred class boundaries and allowed anonymity in public life.

activities.title: 🚤 Gondola at sunset
activities.detail: €80/30min daytime, €100 evening. Gondolas were once everyday transport for Venetian elites; today they survive as one of the last visible symbols of Venice’s canal-based urban life.

activities.title: San Polo sestiere morning walk
activities.detail: Away from tourists. Scuola Grande di San Rocco (Tintoretto, €10). Campo Santa Margherita market: local coffee, focaccia €1.50.

activities.title: Check in: La Grancontessa
activities.detail: ✅ BOOKED RM965.80 total for 2 nights (~RM482.90/night). Good-value Florence stay under your RM500/night target.

activities.title: 🏛️ Piazza della Signoria
activities.detail: Free. Neptune Fountain, Perseus by Cellini, replica David. This square was the civic stage of republican Florence and later Medici rule — effectively the political heart of the city.

activities.title: 🌉 Ponte Vecchio
activities.detail: Free. Florence’s oldest bridge, famous for the shops built along it. The current structure largely dates to the 14th century and survived World War II unlike many other European historic bridges.

activities.title: 🌅 Piazzale Michelangelo at sunset
activities.detail: Walk 20min uphill or Bus 13. Built in the 19th century as part of Florence’s urban redesign, it is not ancient — but it gives the best overview of the Renaissance city and the Arno curve.

activities.title: 🖼️ Uffizi Gallery
activities.detail: ✅ BOOKED for 27 Mar at 09:15. Total €58. The Uffizi began as Medici administrative offices (uffizi = offices) in the 16th century before becoming one of the world’s great Renaissance art collections.

activities.title: ⛪ Duomo complex
activities.detail: Cupola climb €18. Brunelleschi’s dome solved one of the great engineering problems of the Renaissance and became the architectural symbol of Florence’s ambition and wealth.

activities.title: 👨‍🎨 Accademia — Michelangelo's David
activities.detail: ✅ BOOKED for 27 Mar at 16:00. Total €48 for 2 adults.

activities.title: 🛍️ Leather + souvenirs
activities.detail: Scuola del Cuoio (leather school, quality). Procacci on Via de' Tornabuoni: truffle paste €8, best Florence food gift.

activities.title: Check in: Hotel Artemide
activities.detail: ⏳ BOOK NOW. Via Nazionale. Good base for a 3-night Rome stay, near Termini and Repubblica.

activities.title: 🏘️ Trastevere walk
activities.detail: Cross Tiber via Ponte Sisto. Trastevere was historically a working-class district outside Rome’s ancient core and still retains a more lived-in, neighborhood feel than the monumental center.

activities.title: ⛲ Trevi Fountain
activities.detail: Go at quieter hours for fewer crowds. The current fountain is 18th century, built where an ancient Roman aqueduct — the Aqua Virgo — terminated, linking Baroque Rome to imperial infrastructure.

activities.title: 🏛️ Spanish Steps at sunset
activities.detail: 135 steps, completed in the 1720s to connect the Bourbon Spanish embassy below with the French church above — a very Roman mix of diplomacy, urban theater and prestige.

activities.title: 🌙 Piazza Navona evening
activities.detail: Free. The long oval shape comes from the Stadium of Domitian beneath it, so you are effectively standing on the footprint of an ancient Roman arena reworked into Baroque Rome.

activities.title: 🏟️ Colosseum — 8am first slot
activities.detail: ⚠️ BOOK official site. Opened in AD 80 under Titus, the Colosseum was the Flavian amphitheater — Rome’s great arena for spectacles, imperial propaganda and mass entertainment.

activities.title: 🏛️ Roman Forum + Palatine Hill
activities.detail: Included in combo. The Forum was the political, religious and legal center of ancient Rome, while the Palatine became the hill of imperial palaces and elite power.

activities.title: ⛪ Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel
activities.detail: ⚠️ BOOK official site. The museums are the accumulated papal collections; the Sistine Chapel matters not just for Michelangelo’s ceiling, but because it remains the site of the papal conclave.

activities.title: ⛪ St Peter's Basilica (free)
activities.detail: No booking needed. Built over the traditional tomb of St Peter, this is one of the central churches of Catholicism and a statement of papal power in architecture.

activities.title: 🌉 Castel Sant'Angelo at dusk
activities.detail: €15. Originally Emperor Hadrian’s mausoleum, later converted into a papal fortress and refuge — one building that compresses imperial Rome, medieval defense and papal politics together.

food.title: ☕ Arrival drink: Bar & Vini A Pié de Ma'
food.detail: Right at the bottom of the train stairs. Espresso €1.20, local white wine €3. Best spot to shake off the journey. Has focaccia + local anchovies.
food.mapQuery: Bar A Pié de Ma Riomaggiore

food.title: 🐟 Dinner: Il Pescato Cucinato
food.detail: Best value in Cinque Terre. Fried anchovies, calamari, mixed fish plate €10–14pp. Queue outside, eat standing at the counter or nearby steps. Cash only.

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Bar & Vini A Pié de Ma'
food.detail: Cornetto (croissant) €1.30 + cappuccino €1.40. Pack a focaccia slice for the hike (€2). Eat fast — first train to Monterosso at 08:30.
food.mapQuery: Bar A Pie de Ma Riomaggiore

food.title: 🦐 Lunch: Vernazza harbour steps
food.detail: Buy focaccia at Focacceria Vernazza (€2.50) + sit on the harbour steps. OR Gambero Rosso restaurant: spaghetti vongole €16, grilled fish €18. Book Gambero Rosso if you want proper sit-down.

food.title: 🍝 Dinner: Trattoria La Grotta
food.detail: Riomaggiore. Inside a cave — genuinely unique setting. Pesto trofie €13, grilled sea bass €18, house Vermentino €14/bottle. Reserve ahead or arrive before 19:00. Best dinner in Cinque Terre.

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Riomaggiore station bar
food.detail: Quick espresso + cornetto before REG 22910 at 09:55. €2.50 total. Nothing fancy — fuel stop.

food.title: 🍕 Lunch: Milano Centrale food hall
food.detail: You have 20min in Milan (14:20 train). Head to the lower level food hall — Spontini pizza al taglio (€4/slice, legendary in Milan) or grab a tramezzino for the train. Fast, excellent.

food.title: 🥂 Aperitivo: Nilus Bar
food.detail: Lakeside terrace, order a Negroni or Aperol Spritz €5–6. Sit facing the water — Bellagio's lights now fully reflected on the lake. Como mountains as a dark silhouette behind. Perfect 30 min wind-down before dinner.

food.title: 🍝 Dinner: Il Cavatappi
food.detail: Via XX Settembre 10. Risotto al lago (perch fillet) €15, tagliatelle with wild mushrooms €13, lake fish €18. Cosy stone interior, dim lighting — perfect romantic end to the day. If you haven't called ahead: +39 0341 815349. Ask for a window table.

food.title: Breakfast: Heini Luzern
food.detail: Start easy with pastries and coffee before the old-town loop. Order a butter gipfeli or nut pastry + coffee and keep it light after yesterday’s late arrival.

food.title: Lunch: Wirtshaus Galliker
food.detail: Best lunch anchor for today’s Swiss-city plan. Order one classic Swiss plate: veal sausage, schnitzel or rösti. This is the right stop for substance, not just scenery.

food.title: Dinner: Restaurant Pfistern
food.detail: Finish with a proper Swiss old-town dinner in a historic setting. Order rösti or Zürcher Geschnetzeltes and keep it an early, easy finish.

food.title: Breakfast + head to Luzern station
food.detail: Keep breakfast easy and be at Luzern station before 09:00. Train is the right call — no mountain-road stress, no parking issues, better use of the day.

food.title: Lunch in Grindelwald
food.detail: Have a proper village lunch before deciding on the uplift. Pick whatever is open and highly rated on the day — check same-day status before committing.

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Migros bakery, Lucerne Hbf
food.detail: Grab a Gipfeli + coffee at the station Migros (opens 06:30). CHF 5 total. Stock up on Swiss chocolate for the train — last chance for Swiss prices.

food.title: 🥪 Lunch: On the train (packed) or Lugano
food.detail: Best option: pack lunch from Migros. Alternative: 20min stop in Lugano — Bottegone del Vino near station for quick wine + antipasti CHF 15. Train has bistro car — sandwiches CHF 9.

food.title: 🦞 Dinner: All'Arco, Venice (cicchetti)
food.detail: Best cicchetti bar in Venice — Calle dell'Arco near Rialto Market. Bite-size snacks €1.50–3 each — baccalà mantecato, sarde in saor, meatballs. Order 6–8 pieces + ombra wine (€1.50/glass). Real Venice experience.

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Pasticceria Tonolo
food.detail: Best pastry shop in Venice. Dorsoduro 3764. Croissant + cappuccino €3.50. Sfogliatelle, tiramisu, bomboloni — all exceptional. Tiny, no seats — stand at the bar like Venetians. Opens 07:45.

food.title: 🦐 Lunch: All'Arco (or Rialto Market)
food.detail: Return to All'Arco near Rialto for cicchetti lunch OR buy from the Rialto fish market stalls — cured meats, cheese, artichokes, €8–12 for a full spread. Eat on the market steps.

food.title: 🎭 Dinner: Osteria Alle Testiere
food.detail: Venice's best small restaurant — only 24 seats. Book 2 weeks ahead (testiere.it). Tagliolini with shrimp €18, grilled turbot €32, wine pairing €20/person. Worth every euro — you'll remember this meal.

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Venice Santa Lucia station bar
food.detail: Quick espresso + tramezzino (crustless sandwich, €2.50) at the station before Frecciarossa. Bar right inside departures. Nothing special — fuel stop only.

food.title: 🥩 Lunch: Mercato Centrale, Florence
food.detail: Arrive Florence ~12:30. Head straight to Mercato Centrale upstairs food hall (Via dell'Ariento). Best lunch in Florence — lampredotto (tripe) sandwich €5, pasta al ragù €8, fresh pizza €6. Local crowd, no tourists upstairs.

food.title: 🍽️ Dinner: Trattoria Mario, Florence
food.detail: Via Rosina 2, since 1953. Cash only, communal tables, no menu — waiter tells you what's available. Ribollita €7, bistecca Fiorentina (600g) €35, house red €8/litre. Arrive at 19:45 for first seating — queue otherwise.

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Caffè Gilli
food.detail: Historic café since 1733, Piazza della Repubblica. Cornetto di crema €2.20, cappuccino €2. Stand at the bar (not the tables — table service doubles the price). Art Nouveau interior. Instagram moment.

food.title: 🥗 Lunch: Buca Mario
food.detail: Oldest restaurant in Florence (1886). Via Porta Rossa. Pappardelle al cinghiale €14, ribollita €9, grilled Chianina beef €22. More formal than Mario — but the history and vaulted ceilings are worth it.

food.title: 🥩 Dinner: Trattoria Sostanza
food.detail: Famous for butter rigatoni (rigatini al burro) and T-bone steak. Via della Porcellana 25. The butter pasta is life-changing — €12. Bistecca Fiorentina €55 for 2 (shared, minimum 1kg). Book ahead.

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Florence hotel or SMN station
food.detail: Quick breakfast at hotel or grab a cornetto at the Santa Maria Novella station bar before the Frecciarossa. €2.50. Keep it light — Rome has a big lunch waiting.

food.title: 🍕 Lunch: Pizzarium Bonci, Rome
food.detail: Near Vatican (Via della Meloria). Gabriele Bonci's legendary pizza al taglio — sold by weight, €5–8 for a satisfying portion. Best pizza bianca in Rome. Eat standing outside.

food.title: 🍝 Dinner: Ristorante Roscioli
food.detail: Via dei Giubbonari 21. Rome's best pasta — tonnarelli cacio e pepe €16, bucatini all'amatriciana €14. Enormous wine cellar. Book at least 1 week ahead (roscioli.com). If full: Felice a Testaccio (same quality, easier booking).

food.title: ☕ Breakfast: Caffè Sant'Eustachio
food.detail: Piazza di Sant'Eustachio 82 — best espresso in Rome, full stop. Gran caffè speciale €2.20 (pre-sweetened — ask 'senza zucchero' if you want it plain). No seats — stand at bar. 5min walk from Pantheon.

food.title: 🫙 Lunch: Terre e Domus, Roman Forum view
food.detail: Via Sacra (near Forum entrance). Rooftop terrace with direct views of the Colosseum and Forum. Cacio e pepe €14, supplì (fried rice balls) €4 each, local Frascati white €6/glass. Worth the slight premium for the view.

food.title: 🍽️ Dinner: Da Enzo al 29, Trastevere
food.detail: Via dei Vascellari 29. Tiny, 10 tables, always full — book on their site. Cacio e pepe €13, coda alla vaccinara (oxtail stew) €14, carciofi alla romana €7. Authentic Romans eat here. One of Rome's best.

food.title: Final Rome breakfast + check out
food.detail: Keep the morning simple and efficient. Pack the night before and stay focused on the airport transfer.
